title: Energy Markets Are Too Complacent
text:
Energy Markets Are Too Complacent

Tom Holland

Gavekal Research | 25 Jun 2026

On Thursday morning in Asia, front month Brent crude futures were trading at US$72.48/bbl. The price is significant, because on February 27, just hours before the US and Israel began bombing Iran, Brent closed at US$72.48. In other words, with a memorandum of understanding on peace in place and talks scheduled on a longer-term deal, the price of oil has dropped back to where it was on the eve of the conflict. So, is the 2026 energy crisis really over—with relatively little economic harm done?Investors believe so. The proximate cause of the -40% fall in the price of oil since late April is a shift in futures market positioning. Since late May, financial players have cut their long positions in oil futures and accumulated shorts. As a result, the managed money position in Brent futures has fallen from a net long of 400mn bbl at the end of March to 180mn bbl in mid-June.
